sections.title: If the player brings it a bludgeon component before speaking to it

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: I did not ask you to bring me anything, human.

sections.title: Speaking to it again before asking what you can do here

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Yes, human?

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: How curious? I have not seen a creature of your kind for many years. You have ventured into the Nexus human - I should warn you to be aware of the Abyssal Sires.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Ah... Time does not pass in the Abyss at the same rate that it does in your realm, but nevertheless I have been in this place for aeons.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Indeed, when I was last in your realm, the armies of Zamorak were battling the other gods for control, and we sought reinforcements from any plane we could reach.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: That was how I came here. We broke through to the Abyss, seeking allies for our cause, but instead I ended up a prisoner in this place, unable to leave, while the God Wars continued without me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do here?

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: There is something you can do here, human. In fact it may perhaps benefit us both.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Bring me a spine, a claw and an axon from the Abyssal Sires, and I shall construct them into a mighty weapon for you. Yes, we shall both benefit from this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Abyssal Sires.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: The Sires are the engineers of this living pocket universe known to you and I as the Abyss.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: At the hands of the 'Chaos God', Zamorak, like myself, they too were cast down and imprisoned in this living tomb.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: They worked for centuries attempting to escape the pull of the Abyss but as food resources began to deplete they knew that their only chance for survival was to construct this Nexus.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: They absorb flesh and bone of their demon kin, weaving it into the fabric of these very walls. Here they have remained in deep stasis, asleep.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: If you succeed in awakening them, they will no doubt unleash their full fury upon you. Someone of your calibre should be able to deal with them though, especially with the shadows on your side...

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: If you succeed in killing a Sire, you may be fortunate enough to find one of its unsired offspring among its remains. Bring the Unsired here, to the Font of Consumption.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: By placing the Unsired into the Font, I will be able to consume its flesh and perhaps you will receive something worth having.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be off for now.

sections.title: If the player does not have any bludgeon components in their inventory

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Have you harvested any components for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's this stuff for?

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: I shall use the components to construct a mighty weapon for you. Rest assured, the process will be beneficial to both of us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: I still require [bludgeon component(s)] from an Abyssal Sire.

sections.title: If the player has at least one bludgeon component in their inventory

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Have you harvested any components for me?

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: I still require [bludgeon component(s)] from an Abyssal Sire.

sections.title: Speaking to it again after giving it all three components but before completing the final dialogue

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: You have served me well, it is good to see that you humans are still as gullible as ever!

sections.title: Using bludgeon pieces on it after giving it all three components but before completing the final dialogue

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: I do not require anything further from you, human.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Have you harvested any components for me?

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: I now have all the components I require, so here is the weapon I promised you.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: You have served me well, it is good to see that you humans are still as gullible as ever!

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Bringing me those body parts from the Sires has now granted me enough teleportation energy to leave this living hell hole. Perhaps I will pay your realm a visit and there will be much to harvest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yuck! This is disgusting. What use is this? It's unwieldly!

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Have you not learned yet, appearances can be deceiving. You hold in your hands a living weapon whose power is rivalled by few weapons from your world, use it wisely.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Do not think that I am ungrateful for my freedom human. I will leave you this ancient tome that will instruct you in creating more of these weapons in future.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Just use the three components on the book, and it will do the rest. You can get spare copies here any time you need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: Farewell...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I shall use it to slay you, demon.

lines.speaker: The Overseer
lines.text: So be it... I will await you on the fields of blood!
